title: Multi-Source Vertical Sensing of a Winter Dust Event: Quantifying Transport, Microphysics, and Environmental Impacts in Coastal Eastern China
author: Mao, Minjuan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Focus   ing    on    the winter dust event on November    26-   2   8   , 2025,    this study    aims to:    (1) develop and apply a novel quantitative method   for   estimat  ing   the dust input flux; (2) investigate its key microphysical properties; and (3) quantify its multifaceted environmental impacts, including   those   on boundary layer structure, atmospheric acidity, and photochemical processes.
steps_raw: Focus   ing    on    the winter dust event on November    26-   2   8   , 2025,    this study    aims to:    (1) develop and apply a novel quantitative method   for   estimat  ing   the dust input flux; (2) investigate its key microphysical properties; and (3) quantify its multifaceted environmental impacts, including   those   on boundary layer structure, atmospheric acidity, and photochemical processes.
keywords: winter dust; Zhejiang province; microwave radiometer; lidar; wind profiler radar; Dust deposition; Heterogeneous chemistry
